SEARCH
Package de.uka.ilkd.key.proof

Class Proof

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.proof.Proof
All Implemented Interfaces:
Named, ProofObject<Goal>
Direct Known Subclasses:
InfFlowProof
@NullMarked public class Proof extends Object implements ProofObject<Goal>, Named
A proof is represented as a tree whose nodes are created by applying rules on the current (open) goals and dividing them up into several new subgoals. To distinguish between open goals (the ones we are working on) and closed goals or inner nodes we restrict the use of the word goal to open goals which are a subset of the proof tree's leaves. This proof class represents a proof and encapsulates its tree structure. The Goal class represents a goal with all needed extra information, and methods to apply rules. Furthermore, it offers services that deliver the open goals, namespaces and several other information about the current state of the proof.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    List<String>
    keyVersionLog
    when load and save a proof with different versions of key this vector fills up with Strings containing the GIT versions.
    @Nullable List<String>
    userLog
    when different users load and save a proof this vector fills up with Strings containing the usernames.

    • dispose

      public void dispose()
      Cut off all reference such that it does not lead to a big memory leak if someone still holds a reference to this proof object.

    • isDisposed

      public boolean isDisposed()
      Returns true if the proof has been abandoned and the dispose method has been called on this object. Should be asserted before proof object is accessed.

      Specified by:
      openGoals in interface ProofObject<Goal>
      Returns:
      list with the open goals

    • closedGoals

      public ImmutableList<Goal> closedGoals()
      Returns the list of closed goals, needed to make pruning in closed branches possible. If the list needs too much memory, pruning can be disabled via the command line option "--no-pruning-closed". In this case the list will not be filled.
      Returns:
      list with the closed goals

    • allGoals

      public ImmutableList<Goal> allGoals()
      Returns the list of all, open and closed, goals.
      Returns:
      list with all goals.
      See Also:
      • openGoals()
      • closedGoals()

    • openEnabledGoals

      public ImmutableList<Goal> openEnabledGoals()
      return the list of open and enabled goals
      Returns:
      list of open and enabled goals, never null

    • replace

      public void replace(Goal oldGoal, Iterable<Goal> newGoals)
      removes the given goal and adds the new goals in list
      Specified by:
      replace in interface ProofObject<Goal>
      Parameters:
      oldGoal - the old goal that has to be removed from list
      newGoals - the Iterable with the new goals that were result of a rule application on goal

    • closeGoal

      public void closeGoal(Goal goalToClose)
      Close the given goals and all goals in the subtree below it.
      Specified by:
      closeGoal in interface ProofObject<Goal>
      Parameters:
      goalToClose - the goal to close.

    • reOpenGoal

      public void reOpenGoal(Goal goal)
      Opens a previously closed node (the one corresponding to p_goal) and all its closed parents.

      This is, for instance, needed for the MergeRule: In a situation where a merge node and its associated partners have been closed and the merge node is then pruned away, the partners have to be reopened again. Otherwise, we have a soundness issue.

      This will automatically add the goal to the list of open goals.

      Parameters:
      goal - The goal to be opened again.

    • add

      @Deprecated public void add(Goal goal)
      Deprecated.
      use reOpenGoal(Goal) when re-opening a goal
      adds a new goal to the list of goals
      Parameters:
      goal - the Goal to be added

    • add

      public void add(ImmutableList<Goal> goals)
      adds a list with new goals to the list of open goals
      Parameters:
      goals - the IList to be prepended

    • add

      public void add(Iterable<Goal> goals)
      adds a list with new goals to the list of open goals
      Specified by:
      add in interface ProofObject<Goal>
      Parameters:
      goals - the Iterable to be prepended

    • closed

      public boolean closed()
      returns true if the root node is marked as closed and all goals have been removed
      Specified by:
      closed in interface ProofObject<Goal>

    • setStepIndices

      public void setStepIndices()
      For all nodes in this proof: set the step index according to their position in the tree.

    • pruneProof

      public void pruneProof(Goal goal)
      Performs an undo operation on the given goal. This is equivalent to a pruning of the parent node of the goal (if this parent node exists).
      Parameters:
      goal - the Goal where the last rule application gets undone

    • pruneProof

      public ImmutableList<Node> pruneProof(Node cuttingPoint)
      Prunes the subtree beneath the node cuttingPoint, i.e. the node cuttingPoint remains as the last node on the branch. As a result, an open goal is associated with this node.
      Parameters:
      cuttingPoint - node below which to prune
      Returns:
      the subtrees that have been pruned.

    • pruneProof

      public ImmutableList<Node> pruneProof(Node cuttingPoint, boolean fireChanges)

    • breadthFirstSearch

      public void breadthFirstSearch(Node startNode, ProofVisitor visitor)
      Makes a downwards directed breadth first search on the proof tree, starting with node startNode. The visited notes are reported to the object visitor. The first reported node is startNode.

    • findAny

      public @Nullable Node findAny(@NonNull Predicate<Node> pred)
      Bread-first search for the first node, that matches the given predicate.
      Parameters:
      pred - non-null test function
      Returns:
      a node fulfilling pred or null

      fires the event that new goals have been added to the list of goals

    • fireProofGoalsChanged

      public void fireProofGoalsChanged()
      fires the event that the proof has been restructured

    • fireProofClosed

      protected void fireProofClosed()
      fires the event that the proof has closed. This event fired instead of the proofGoalRemoved event when the last goal in list is removed.

      protected void fireNotesChanged(Node node)

      public Goal getOpenGoal(@NonNull Node node)
      returns the goal that belongs to the given node or null if the node is an inner one

      the goal that belongs to the given node or null if the node is an inner one

    • isClosedGoal

      public boolean isClosedGoal(Node node)
      Parameters:
      node - the Node which is checked for a corresponding closed goal
      Returns:
      true if the goal that belongs to the given node is closed and false if not or if there is no such goal.

    • getClosedGoal

      public Goal getClosedGoal(Node node)
      Get the closed goal belonging to the given node if it exists.
      Parameters:
      node - the Node where a corresponding closed goal is searched
      Returns:
      the closed goal that belongs to the given node or null if the node is an inner one or an open goal

      public ImmutableList<Goal> getSubtreeGoals(Node node)
      returns the list of goals of the subtree starting with node.
      Parameters:
      node - the Node where to start from
      Returns:
      the list of goals of the subtree starting with node

    • getClosedSubtreeGoals

      public ImmutableList<Goal> getClosedSubtreeGoals(Node node)
      Returns a list of all (closed) goals of the closed subtree pending from this node.
      Parameters:
      node - the root of the subtree
      Returns:
      the closed goals in the subtree

      public ImmutableList<Goal> getSubtreeEnabledGoals(Node node)

      Returns:

    • find

      returns true iff the given node is found in the proof tree
      Parameters:

      Returns:
      true iff the given node is found in the proof tree

    • countNodes

      public int countNodes()
      retrieves number of nodes

    • setRuleAppIndexToAutoMode

      public void setRuleAppIndexToAutoMode()
      Currently the rule app index can either operate in interactive mode (and contain applications of all existing taclets) or in automatic mode (and only contain a restricted set of taclets that can possibly be applied automated). This distinction could be replaced with a more general way to control the contents of the rule app index

    • setRuleAppIndexToInteractiveMode

      public void setRuleAppIndexToInteractiveMode()

    • countBranches

      public int countBranches()
      retrieves number of branches

    • getStatistics

      public Statistics getStatistics()
      Retrieves a bunch of statistics to the proof tree. This implementation traverses the proof tree only once. Statistics are not cached; don't call this method too often.

      public String toString()
      toString
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object

      Retrieves a user-defined data.
      Type Parameters:
      T - any class
      Parameters:
      service - the class for which the data were registered
      Returns:
      null or the previous data
      See Also:
      • register(Object, Class)

    • register

      public <T> void register(T obj, Class<T> service)
      Register a user-defined data in this node info.
      Type Parameters:
      T - type of the object to register
      Parameters:
      obj - an object to be registered
      service - the key under it should be registered

    • deregister

      public <T> void deregister(T obj, Class<T> service)
      Remove a previous registered user-defined data.
      Type Parameters:
      T - type of the object to unregister
      Parameters:

      service - the key under which the data was registered

      public @NonNull Lookup getUserData()

      Returns:

    • setMutedProofCloseEvents

      public void setMutedProofCloseEvents(boolean mutedProofCloseEvents)

    • copyCachedGoals

      public void copyCachedGoals(Proof referencedFrom, @Nullable Consumer<Integer> callbackTotal, @Nullable Runnable callbackBranch)
      For each branch closed by reference to another proof, copy the relevant proof steps into this proof.
      Parameters:
      referencedFrom - filter, if not null copy only from that proof
      callbackTotal - callback that gets the total number of branches to complete
      callbackBranch - callback notified every time a branch has been copied

      public void printSymbols(PrintWriter ps)

    • getCreationTime
